```json
{
    "cefr_level": "B1",
    "grammar_score": 0.55,
    "corrections": [
        "simular -> similar",
        "Here is the tips -> Here are the tips",
        "this apps -> these apps",
        "spend a lot of time to TV -> spend a lot of time watching TV / on TV",
        "leave from useless groups -> leave useless groups / unsubscribe from useless groups",
        "enought -> enough",
        "write me at personal messages -> write to me in a personal message / message me privately"
    ],
    "feedback": "You have successfully completed the task, providing clear and relevant advice in a well-structured format. Your vocabulary is appropriate for the topic (e.g., 'Manage your time', 'social media', 'energy'). To improve, focus on basic grammar accuracy, particularly subject-verb agreement ('Here are the tips') and plural forms ('these apps'). Also, review prepositions and verb patterns after 'spend time' (e.g., 'spend time on TV' or 'watching TV')."
}
```

{
    "cefr_level": "B2",

        "decition -> decision",
        "tips how to get it -> tips on how to get it",
        "options how to organize -> options on how to organize / options for organizing",
        "each my day -> each of my days / my day",
        "other's works -> others' work",
        "But very important thing -> But a very important thing"
    ],
    "feedback": "This is a strong B2 response. You have effectively organized your ideas using a range of cohesive devices (However, Firstly, For example, Also, though). The vocabulary is varied and precise for the topic (resources, overload, assignments, periods of free time, abuse help). There are some grammatical slips, particularly with determiners ('each my day') and prepositions ('tips how to'), and a spelling error ('decition'), but these do not impede communication or detract from the overall quality of the argument. To reach C1, focus on eliminating minor errors in article usage and preposition selection."
}
```

```json
{
    "cefr_level": "B1",
    "grammar_score": 0.45,
    "corrections": [
        "I want to tell you about two of my own tips, which help me...",
        "Firstly, I recommend communicating more with other people.",
        "They can help you look at life simply.",
        "In the future, good networking will give you a lot of opportunities.",
        "Secondly, you need to count your time; do time management.",
        "If you think that you study all the time and don't have free time - try noting your day.",
        "In my experience, all guys who do this find some bad habits that really take a lot of time.",
        "Delete them and live more freely.",
        "I believe my tips will help you."
    ],
    "feedback": "You have successfully organized your response using linking words like 'Firstly' and 'Secondly', which is great for a B1 level text. You also attempted complex sentence structures and used good vocabulary items such as 'networking', 'opportunity', and 'motivation'. However, there are frequent spelling errors (e.g., 'futcher', 'beleave', 'delite') and grammar mistakes with verb patterns and prepositions. Focus on checking your spelling and reviewing basic verb forms to improve clarity."
}
```

```json
{
    "cefr_level": "B1",
    "grammar_score": 0.5,
    "corrections": [
        "It is easily. -> It is easy.",
        "Now I want to some tips for became diffiecult study easilier. -> Now I want to give some tips to make difficult study easier.",
        "It is not obviously tip -> It is not an obvious tip",
        "your brain relax and do not do anything -> your brain relaxes and does not do anything",
        "It is obviously. -> It is obvious."
    ],
    "feedback": "The text addresses the task well and provides clear, logical advice, demonstrating an ability to link sentences into a coherent argument (e.g., explaining the benefits of sport and sleep). The vocabulary is ambitious for this level, with attempts to use words like 'solution', 'graduate', 'approximately', and 'iteration', which is commendable. However, the score is limited by frequent basic grammar errors, particularly the confusion between adjectives and adverbs ('It is easily', 'obviously tip'), subject-verb agreement ('brain relax'), and non-standard word formation ('easilier'). While the complexity of the sentences suggests a move towards B2, the density of elementary errors confirms a B1 classification."
}
```

{
    "cefr_level": "B1",

    "corrections": [

        "knowledge that university gives is complex -> the knowledge provided by the university is comprehensive"
    ],
    "feedback": "This is a strong B1 essay that communicates ideas clearly and effectively. The essay is well-organized with a clear introduction, body paragraphs, and conclusion. To reach B2, focus on using a wider variety of vocabulary to avoid repetition (e.g., instead of repeating 'complex', try 'comprehensive', 'specialized', or 'advanced'). }
```

    "cefr_level": "B2",

While there are some spelling and mechanical errors (spacing and typos like 'talant'), they do not impede communication. To reach the next level, focus on more precise collocations (e.g., 'In my view' instead of 'On my point of view') and ensure careful proofreading for spacing errors."
